author: HonestJoe
post:
$TSLA Bloomberg) -- Tesla Inc.’s European sales slump carried over into the new year, with registrations plummeting in two of the region’s leading markets for battery-electric vehicles. 
 
France was the third-largest market for EV registrations in Europe last year, trailing only Germany and the UK. In January, Tesla sold fewer vehicles there than Volkswagen AG’s Cupra or Stellantis NV’s Jeep brands. 
 
The major exception to Tesla’s trend of lower sales in Europe last year was Norway, where its registrations climbed 41% in part due to consumers rushing purchases before a policy change took effect. The Norwegian government last month tightened rules for value-added tax exemptions, sending total industry sales down 76% in January. 
 
Across Europe in 2025, Tesla’s sales fell 27% even as industrywide battery-electric vehicle registrations jumped 30%, according to the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association.

post:
$TSLA People miss the real problem FSD solves. 
 
Most people don’t live near their jobs because they can’t afford to. The trade-off has always been brutal: cheaper housing = longer, soul-sucking commutes. 
 
FSD flips that equation. 
 
Commute time becomes productive time. Work, read, plan your day — or just decompress. You get home less stressed, less exhausted, and in a better mood for your kids and family. 
 
That’s not a gadget. That’s quality of life. 
That’s time back. 
That’s mental health. 
 
This is why autonomy gets adopted fast. Not because it’s cool — because it quietly makes everyday life better for millions.

Speculation is growing that Elon Musk could merge SpaceX and his AI startup xAI ahead of a potential mid-year IPO for SpaceX, reviving hopes of deeper AI convergence across Musk’s companies, including Tesla. SpaceX is seeking to expand its AI ambitions, including plans to build data-center capacity and a proposed “space cloud” of up to one million satellites—around 100 times larger than Starlink—to support AI computing. 
A merger could make strategic sense as SpaceX increasingly positions itself as an AI infrastructure player, while xAI has been valued near $200 billion in recent funding rounds. However, analysts warn that a full convergence involving Tesla faces a major valuation mismatch. Tesla trades at roughly 200 times estimated 2026 earnings, while SpaceX could be valued closer to 400 times earnings, potentially forcing significant share dilution that many Tesla shareholders may resist. 
$TSLA $SPX $DJIA $SPACEX

Elon Musk is in advanced talks to combine SpaceX with xAI, highlighting how AI’s funding needs are pushing more “empire consolidation” moves.
